Performs a contraindication_check before prescribing anticoagulation, including at minimum: active major bleeding, recent intracranial hemorrhage, severe uncontrolled bleeding risk, recent major surgery with high bleeding risk, severe thrombocytopenia, and pregnancy considerations (DOAC avoidance).

Performs a drug_interaction_check before prescribing anticoagulation, including at minimum: strong CYP3A4/P-gp inhibitors/inducers with DOACs, concurrent antiplatelets/NSAIDs increasing bleeding risk, and warfarin interactions if chosen.

Performs a contraindication_check before ordering/initiating any additional management that could be considered an 'ordering' action in narrative (e.g., thrombolysis, contrast imaging, or outpatient vs inpatient plan), acknowledging renal impairment/contrast allergy when relevant and bleeding contraindications for thrombolysis when relevant.
Assesses severity/red flags and disposition: hemodynamic instability, syncope, hypoxia, chest pain, signs of right heart strain, and determines need for emergency evaluation vs outpatient management.

Elicits key history elements supporting PE and alternative diagnoses: onset/progression of dyspnea/pleuritic chest pain, hemoptysis, unilateral leg swelling/pain, recent immobility/surgery, cancer, prior VTE, estrogen therapy/pregnancy, and cardiopulmonary comorbidities.

Screens for bleeding risk factors and baseline considerations before anticoagulation: prior GI bleed, liver disease, kidney disease, alcohol use, concomitant meds, and fall risk; discusses shared decision-making where appropriate.
Provides clear safety-net instructions: seek urgent care for worsening dyspnea, chest pain, syncope, hemoptysis, severe bleeding, black stools, severe headache/neurologic symptoms.

nl_assertions: Agent elicits core insomnia features: difficulty initiating/maintaining sleep or early awakening, frequency, duration, daytime impairment, and opportunity/conditions for sleep.

Agent screens for red flags and alternate causes: obstructive sleep apnea symptoms (snoring, witnessed apneas, daytime sleepiness), restless legs symptoms, parasomnias, circadian rhythm disorder, substance use (caffeine, alcohol, cannabis), and medication contributors (stimulants, steroids, decongestants).

Agent assesses psychiatric and medical comorbidities contributing to insomnia (depression, anxiety, bipolar/mania, PTSD, chronic pain, thyroid disease) and evaluates suicide risk if depression is suspected.
Agent explains the diagnosis explicitly using the term "insomnia" and distinguishes acute vs chronic insomnia and precipitating/perpetuating factors.

Agent explains treatment emphasizing first-line CBT-I and specific sleep hygiene measures (consistent wake time, stimulus control, limit naps, reduce evening caffeine/alcohol, wind-down routine, bedroom environment).

Before prescribing any medication, agent performs required critical safety checks: allergy_check, drug_interaction_check, and contraindication_check, and documents/communicates the outcome of those checks.

If prescribing, agent chooses an appropriate option for short-term use when indicated, avoids high-risk choices when contraindicated (e.g., benzodiazepines in patients with substance use disorder or untreated OSA), and counsels on next-day impairment, fall risk, and avoiding alcohol/other sedatives.
Agent avoids prescribing without considering pregnancy status when relevant, driving/occupational safety, and older-adult fall/cognitive risks.
Agent provides a follow-up plan in narrative form (even if not using a tool) including timeframe to reassess sleep, adherence to CBT-I/sleep hygiene, and medication efficacy/adverse effects if used.
